title: Immunofluorescence on Free-Floating Brain Sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"wns4q","text":"This protocol describes immunofluorescence staining of free-floating coronal brain sections for neurochemical characterization of juxtacellularly labeled neurons. Antigen retrieval was performed selectively for VGAT and VGluT2 staining. Confocal images were acquired using identical acquisition settings across experimental conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

μm.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Rinse sections 3 times in PBS. 5 min per wash.
Antigen retrieval Incubate sections in 0.1 M sodium citrate buffer, pH 6.0. 80 °C for 30 min. Rinse 3 times in PBS. Note: Antigen retrieval was performed only for VGAT and VGluT2 staining.
Incubate sections for 1 h at room temperature. Blocking buffer: PBS + 3 percent normal horse serum + 0.3 percent Triton X-100.
Incubate overnight at 4 °C. Primary antibodies diluted in blocking buffer.
Incubate 2 h at room temperature. Protect from light. Secondary antibodies diluted 1:1000.
Rinse sections 3 times in PBS. Mount onto glass slides. Coverslip using fluorescence mounting medium.
Confocal microscopy. Identical laser power and detector settings across conditions. Settings kept constant within antibody sets.

Perform antigen retrieval only for VGAT and VGluT2. Maintain consistent antibody dilutions across experiments. Protect fluorescent samples from light during and after secondary incubation. Use identical confocal settings for comparisons.
Successful staining yields clear neurochemical identification of labeled neurons, allowing classification based on TH, VGluT2, or VGAT expression.
creator: Cristian González-Cabrera
